1.5: Like the victim who gorges himself, like animals that are fattened for sacrifice, like animals that eat and drink normally on the very day they are offered as a sacrifice.
1.6: ); or, because while praying, he anointed him. This passage expresses a clear promulgation of the sacrament of Extreme Unction instituted by Jesus Christ.
1.22: . ― Ignite the course of our lives : she makes us sin throughout our lives, herself being excited by the spirit of lies, the demon.
1.24: For Saint James, Abraham is the representative and type of all true believers: the conclusion is therefore legitimate. His doctrine is also consistent with that of Saint Paul, who only values "« faith working through works »" (see Galatians ,
1.8: . The hand includes exterior works; the heart , passions.
1.4: Sabaoth . See, on the virtue of this word, Romans ,
1.16: To each other, that the sick person should make a humble confession of his sins and wrongs before his brothers, cf. Matthew
1.19: . — When this letter was written, there were Jews who believed that violating the law on one point or a few points and observing it on all others was not a grave sin that could incur God's wrath, that there was even some merit in it. Saint Augustine says that this was also the error of some Christians of his time. It is against this error, therefore, that Saint James speaks out; and when he says all , The reason is that he considers the law as a whole. Thus, whether one violates this or that particular precept, it is always the law itself that is violated.
1.15: ), that is to say, she hates to death those who are an obstacle to her, and envy , etc.
1.2: A vivid portrayal of the turmoil of a soul that cannot restrain its desires: she covet a thousand things, and not obtaining them, she becomes murderous (in her heart, see
1.31: . Rahab… receiving in Jericho spies of Joshua.
1.14: that they pray over him . The apostle uses this expression because, during prayer, the priest held his hand outstretched over the sick person. (Cf. Matthew
1.20: Will save a soul from death ; that of the sinner. ― Will cover , etc. He will erase the sins of the one he converts by leading him to do penance and to confess; and his own, because by thus exercising charity, he makes himself worthy to receive the grace of the remission of his faults.
1.11: What end did the Lord have in store for him?, the happy ending that the Lord granted to Job.

2.1: My brothers, do not mix favoritism towards certain people with faith in Jesus Christ our Lord of glory.
2.2: If, for example, a man wearing a gold ring and fine clothes enters your assembly, and a poor man in filthy clothes also enters
2.3: And turning your gaze upon the one who is magnificently dressed, you say to him, "You, sit here in this place of honor," and you say to the poor man, "You, stand there, or sit here at the footstool of my feet."«
2.4: Isn't that making distinctions between yourselves and setting yourselves up as judges with perverse thoughts?
2.5: Listen, my beloved brothers: Has not God chosen those who are poor in the eyes of the world to be rich in faith and heirs of the kingdom He promised to those who love Him?
2.6: And you, you are insulting the poor. Aren't the rich the ones oppressing you and dragging you into court?
2.7: Are they not the ones who insult the beautiful name you bear?
2.8: If you fulfill the royal law, according to this passage of Scripture: «You shall love your neighbor as yourself,» you are doing well
2.9: But if you show favoritism, you commit a sin, and the law itself condemns you as transgressors.,
2.10: for whoever has kept the whole law, and yet fails in one point, is guilty of all of it.
2.11: Indeed, he who said, «You shall not commit adultery,» also said, «You shall not murder.» So if you murder, even though you do not commit adultery, you are a transgressor of the law.
2.12: Speak and act as if you were to be judged by the law of liberty
2.13: because judgment will be without mercy for the one who has not shown mercy; mercy triumphs over judgment.
2.14: What good is it, my brothers, for a man to say he has faith if he does not have works? Can such faith save him?
2.15: If a brother or sister is naked and does not have their daily necessities of food, and one of you says to them
2.16: «"Go in peace, be warmed and filled" without giving them what their bodies need, what good is it?
2.17: The same is true of faith: if it does not have works, it is dead in itself.
2.18: But one could even say: "You have faith and I have works." "Show me your faith without works and I will show you my faith by my works."«
2.19: You believe that there is only one God, you are right, the demons believe it too and they tremble.
2.20: But do you want to convince yourself, O foolish man, that faith without works is without virtue?
2.21: Was not Abraham, our father, justified by works when he offered his son Isaac on the altar?
2.22: You see that faith cooperated with his works, and that by his works his faith was made perfect.
2.23: And the Scripture was fulfilled, "Abraham believed God, and it was due to him that he was considered righteous," and he was called a friend of God.
2.24: You see that a man is justified by works and not by faith alone.
2.25: Similarly, was not Rahab, the courtesan, justified by her works when she received Joshua's messengers and sent them away by another route?
2.26: Just as the body without the soul is dead, so faith without works is dead.

3.1: My brothers, not so many of you should become teachers, knowing that we will be judged more strictly.
3.2: For we all sin in many ways. If anyone does not sin in what he says, he is a perfect man, able to bridle his whole body also.
3.3: If we put a bit in a horse's mouth to make it obey us, we also govern its entire body.
3.4: See also the ships, large as they are, and although driven by impetuous winds, they are guided by a very small rudder at the will of the pilot who steers them.
3.5: Thus, the tongue is a very small part of the body, but what great things it can boast of! See, a spark can set a great forest ablaze.
3.6: The tongue, too, is a fire, a world of iniquity. Being only one of our members, the tongue is capable of infecting the whole body; it sets the course of our life ablaze, itself ablaze with the fire of hell.
3.7: All species of quadrupeds, birds, reptiles, and marine animals can be tamed and have been tamed by humans.,
3.8: But the tongue, no man can tame it. It is a scourge that cannot be stopped, it is filled with deadly poison.
3.9: With it we bless the Lord and our Father, and with it we curse men, who have been made in the image of God.
3.10: From the same mouth come both curse and blessing. It should not be so, my brothers.
3.11: Does the same opening give rise to both sweet and bitter?
3.12: Can a fig tree, my brothers, produce olives, or a grapevine figs? Similarly, a salt spring cannot produce fresh water.
3.13: Who among you is wise and intelligent? Let him show his moderation and wisdom in the course of a good life.
3.14: But if you have bitter zeal and selfish ambition in your hearts, do not boast or lie against the truth.
3.15: Such wisdom does not descend from above; it is earthly, carnal, diabolical.
3.16: For where there is jealousy and rivalry, there is trouble and every evil practice.
3.17: But the wisdom from above is first of all pure, then peaceable, condescending, conciliatory, full of mercy and good fruits, impartial, and without hypocrisy.
3.18: The fruit of righteousness is sown in peace by those who practice peace.

4.1: Where do wars and struggles among you come from? Do they not come from your passions that fight within you?
4.2: You covet and you do not have, you are murderers, you are jealous and you cannot obtain, you are in a state of struggle and war and you do not obtain, because you do not ask.
4.3: You ask and you do not receive, because you ask wrongly, with the intention of satisfying your passions.
4.4: Adulterers, do you not know that friendship with the world is enmity with God? Whoever wishes to be a friend of the world makes himself an enemy of God.
4.5: Or do you think that Scripture says in vain, «The Spirit who has put in you loves you even to the point of jealousy»?»
4.6: But he gives even greater grace, according to what Scripture says: «God opposes the proud but gives grace to the humble.»
4.7: Submit yourselves therefore to God, resist the devil and he will flee from you.
4.8: Draw near to God and he will draw near to you. Cleanse your hands, sinners, purify your hearts, double-minded people.
4.9: Feel your misery, grieve and weep, let your laughter turn to tears and your joy to sadness.
4.10: Humble yourselves before the Lord and he will exalt you. Brothers, do not speak evil of one another.
4.11: Anyone who speaks evil of their brother or judges their brother speaks evil of the law and judges the law. But if you judge the law, you are no longer a doer of the law but a judge of it.
4.12: There is only one lawgiver and one judge, the one who has the power to save and to destroy. But who are you, you who judge your neighbor?
4.13: Well then, you who say, "Today or tomorrow we will go to such and such a city, stay there for a year, do business and make money,",
4.14: You who do not know what will happen tomorrow, because what is your life?
4.15: You are a vapor that appears for a moment and then vanishes, instead of saying, "If the Lord wills" or "If we are alive, we will do this or that,"«
4.16: But now you boast in your presumption. All such boasting is wrong.
4.17: Therefore, whoever knows how to do what is right and does not do it, commits a sin.

5.1: Now it's your turn, you rich people. Weep, burst into tears at the sight of the miseries that will befall you.
5.2: Your wealth has rotted away and your clothes have been eaten by worms.
5.3: Your gold and silver have corroded, and their corrosion will testify against you, and like fire will consume your flesh. You have amassed wealth in the last days.
5.4: Behold, he cries out against you, the wages with which you failed to pay the workers who mowed your fields, and the cries of the harvesters have reached the ears of the Lord of Hosts.
5.5: You have lived on earth in luxury and feasting; you have been like the victim who gorges himself on the day he is to be slaughtered.
5.6: You have condemned, you have killed the righteous man, he does not resist you.
5.7: Therefore, be patient, my brothers, until the coming of the Lord. See how the farmer, hoping for the precious fruit of the earth, waits patiently until he receives the autumn rain and the spring rain.
5.8: You too, be patient and strengthen your hearts, for the coming of the Lord is near.
5.9: Brothers, do not start complaining to one another, lest you be judged: behold, the Judge is standing at the door.
5.10: As an example of generosity in trials and of patience, brothers, take the prophets who spoke in the name of the Lord.
5.11: See, we proclaim blessed those who have suffered. You have heard of the patience of Job and have seen what end the Lord brought about for him, for the Lord is full of compassion and mercy.
5.12: Above all, my brothers, do not swear by heaven or earth or by any other oath, but let your yes be yes and your no be no, so that you will not fall under judgment.
5.13: Is anyone among you in distress? Let him pray. Is anyone in joy? Let him sing hymns.
5.14: Is anyone among you sick? Let them call the priests of the church to pray over them and anoint them with oil in the name of the Lord.
5.15: And the prayer of faith will save the sick person, and the Lord will restore him, and if he has committed sins, they will be forgiven him.
5.16: Therefore confess your sins to one another and pray for one another, that you may be healed, for the prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective.
5.17: Elijah was a man subject to the same miseries as we are: He prayed earnestly that it would not rain, and it did not rain on the land for three years and six months.,
5.18: He prayed again and the sky gave rain and the earth produced its fruits.
5.19: My brothers, if any of you has drifted away from the truth and someone brings him back,
5.20: Know that whoever brings a sinner back from the way in which he strays will save a soul from death and cover a multitude of sins.
